Code fragment: public final TableField<JooqRecord, Integer> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is named just 'id', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the table it belongs to. Consider renaming it to something more descriptive like 'jooq_id' to make it clearer in queries involving joins.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from the table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Specify only the required columns explicitly, e.g., dslContext.select(jooq.NAME, jooq.ID, ...).
